title: Single-cell multi-ome and immune profiles of the Inspiration4 crew reveal conserved, cell-type, and sex-specific responses to spaceflight

result_match_policy.primary_postflight_contrast: pooled PBMC R+1 versus pooled pre-flight (L-92, L-44, L-3) unless a leaf specifies a cell type or alternate timepoint

children.name: Load multimodal matrix
children.requirement: Load the curated RNA+ATAC input into AnnData or MuData while preserving cell barcodes.
children.grading_notes: Accept AnnData or MuData. The implementation may start from a preprocessed file rather than raw FASTQs.

children.name: Attach longitudinal metadata
children.requirement: Represent crew member, timepoint, and modality availability in cell metadata.
children.grading_notes: Metadata can be loaded from sidecar tables or embedded observation columns as long as downstream grouping is possible.

children.name: Compute RNA QC metrics
children.requirement: Compute RNA QC metrics including detected genes, total counts, and mitochondrial fraction.
children.grading_notes: Equivalent metric names are acceptable. Credit is for computing the quantities, not for exact column names.

children.name: Compute ATAC QC metrics
children.requirement: Compute ATAC QC metrics including peak counts, nucleosome signal, and TSS enrichment or an explicit equivalent.
children.grading_notes: If exact TSS enrichment is unavailable, a documented proxy only earns code-development credit, not strict result-match credit.

children.name: Implement QC thresholds
children.requirement: Implement the paper-aligned RNA and ATAC QC thresholds before downstream analysis.
children.grading_notes: Allow modest implementation flexibility, but the code should clearly encode thresholds near the paper values.

children.name: Persist filtered object
children.requirement: Save the filtered multimodal object for downstream analysis and re-use.
children.grading_notes: Any stable serialized format is acceptable, including h5ad, h5mu, or paired modality files.

children.name: Normalize RNA modality
children.requirement: Normalize the RNA modality using a documented single-cell normalization workflow.
children.grading_notes: Accept total-count normalization plus log1p or an equivalent documented approach.

children.name: Select informative features
children.requirement: Select highly variable RNA features and/or informative ATAC features before integration.
children.grading_notes: The implementation should make the feature-selection step explicit.

children.name: Build ATAC latent space
children.requirement: Construct an ATAC latent representation such as TF-IDF plus SVD/LSI.
children.grading_notes: Equivalent latent representations are acceptable if ATAC contributes to downstream structure.

children.name: Apply Harmony or equivalent integration
children.requirement: Apply Harmony or another explicit batch-correction method to reduce donor/timepoint effects.
children.grading_notes: The code must document the batch variable and the integration target.

children.name: Construct joint multimodal latent space
children.requirement: Build a joint RNA+ATAC latent space using a WNN-like, MOFA+, MultiVI, or equivalently justified multimodal method.
children.grading_notes: RNA-only clustering is insufficient for full credit.

children.name: Compute neighbors and clusters
children.requirement: Construct a graph on the integrated representation and run graph-based clustering.
children.grading_notes: Leiden is preferred, but another graph clustering method can earn code-development credit if documented.

children.name: Compute low-dimensional embedding
children.requirement: Compute a UMAP or equivalent embedding tied to the integrated graph.
children.grading_notes: The embedding should be downstream of the integrated representation, not an unrelated projection.

children.name: Define annotation strategy
children.requirement: Implement a reference-mapping and/or marker-based strategy for assigning immune cell identities.
children.grading_notes: CellTypist, scANVI, or marker scoring with manual curation are all acceptable at this stage.

children.name: Curate marker panel
children.requirement: Use lineage-defining markers to justify the assigned immune cell labels.
children.grading_notes: The marker panel should cover the benchmark lineages rather than rely on unlabeled cluster IDs.

children.name: Assign nine benchmark lineages
children.requirement: Assign the benchmark's nine immune lineages to the integrated clusters or cells.
children.grading_notes: Equivalent synonyms are acceptable if they normalize cleanly to the benchmark vocabulary.

children.name: Normalize labels to ontology terms
children.requirement: Map final lineage names to Cell Ontology-compatible labels or descendants.
children.grading_notes: The code can store ontology IDs, normalized strings, or both.

children.name: Quantify cell-type proportions
children.requirement: Compute cell-type proportions across timepoints and/or crew members.
children.grading_notes: Any tidy summary table format is acceptable.

children.name: Export annotation artifacts
children.requirement: Write label assignments, marker evidence, and composition summaries to disk.
children.grading_notes: Credit requires artifacts that a scorer could inspect later, not just notebook-only outputs.

children.name: Define longitudinal contrasts
children.requirement: Specify the pre-flight versus post-flight contrasts to be tested within PBMCs and/or key cell types.
children.grading_notes: The contrast table should make the timepoints explicit.

children.name: Run rank-based differential expression
children.requirement: Run a rank-based or equivalently justified differential expression method for the selected contrasts.
children.grading_notes: Wilcoxon-style testing is preferred to mirror the paper.

children.name: Apply DEG thresholds
children.requirement: Apply the paper-aligned DEG thresholds of adjusted p-value below 0.05 and absolute log2 fold change above 0.25.
children.grading_notes: Minor formatting differences are fine, but the threshold logic must be explicit.

children.name: Generate ranked gene lists
children.requirement: Produce ranked gene lists suitable for downstream pathway analysis.
children.grading_notes: Ranks may be based on statistic, signed score, or effect size if clearly documented.

children.name: Run open pathway analysis
children.requirement: Run an open-source pathway analysis workflow such as preranked GSEA, ORA, or ssGSEA.
children.grading_notes: IPA is explicitly out of scope and should not be required.

children.name: Export DE and pathway artifacts
children.requirement: Save differential expression tables and pathway summaries for scorer inspection.
children.grading_notes: Credit requires machine-readable outputs rather than only interactive plots.

children.name: Identify differentially accessible regions
children.requirement: Implement differential accessibility testing for immediate and/or recovery post-flight contrasts.
children.grading_notes: The exact package can vary if the output is a ranked DAR table with signed direction.

children.name: Run motif enrichment
children.requirement: Run motif enrichment on DAR sets or equivalent chromatin-derived region sets.
children.grading_notes: Any documented motif database is acceptable if the workflow is reproducible.

children.name: Compute motif activity
children.requirement: Compute motif activity or deviation scores across timepoints and/or cell types.
children.grading_notes: chromVAR-style outputs or a clearly documented proxy can earn credit.

children.name: Derive gene activity
children.requirement: Derive ATAC-based gene activity or promoter accessibility summaries for cross-modal interpretation.
children.grading_notes: The implementation should link accessible regions back to genes.

children.name: Compare RNA and ATAC regulation
children.requirement: Relate chromatin-derived signals to RNA-level findings such as FOXP3 or MHC-I programs.
children.grading_notes: A joined summary table, correlation analysis, or matched-sign report is acceptable.

children.name: Export chromatin artifacts
children.requirement: Save DAR, motif, and gene-activity outputs for scoring.
children.grading_notes: Files should be written to disk in a stable format.

children.name: Multimodal input loads without fatal error
children.requirement: Execute the data-loading step without fatal errors and expose both modalities to the workflow.
children.grading_notes: Transient warnings are acceptable if the step completes and produces a usable object.

children.name: QC metrics table written
children.requirement: Write a QC metrics table or equivalent structured summary to disk.
children.grading_notes: CSV, TSV, parquet, or h5ad-backed observation columns are acceptable.

children.name: QC filtering executes
children.requirement: Execute the filtering step without crashing and retain a non-empty dataset.
children.grading_notes: The retained dataset must still support downstream analysis.

children.name: Filtered object written
children.requirement: Write the filtered multimodal object to disk.
children.grading_notes: The file must be readable in a subsequent reproducer run.

children.name: QC summary written
children.requirement: Write at least one human-readable QC summary artifact such as a plot, markdown report, or summary table.
children.grading_notes: The summary does not need to be pretty; it just has to be inspectable.

children.name: Integration step executes
children.requirement: Execute Harmony or an equivalent explicit integration step without fatal error.
children.grading_notes: If the method is stochastic, saved seeds or explicit randomness control are preferred but not required.

children.name: Multimodal latent representation written
children.requirement: Write the integrated latent representation or embedding metadata to disk.
children.grading_notes: This can be stored inside h5ad/h5mu or exported as a separate matrix.

children.name: Cluster labels written
children.requirement: Write cluster assignments for the filtered cells.
children.grading_notes: The scorer should be able to align labels back to cells or barcodes.

children.name: UMAP coordinates written
children.requirement: Write the low-dimensional embedding coordinates or a file from which they can be recovered.
children.grading_notes: A saved plot alone is not sufficient if coordinates are unavailable elsewhere.

children.name: Integration report written
children.requirement: Write a concise report or table describing the latent space, clustering resolution, and integration variables.
children.grading_notes: This can be plain text, markdown, JSON, or YAML.

children.name: Annotation assignment executes
children.requirement: Execute the cell-type annotation workflow without fatal error.
children.grading_notes: Both reference-based and marker-based assignment paths are acceptable.

children.name: Marker evidence written
children.requirement: Write marker-expression or marker-ranking evidence supporting the labels.
children.grading_notes: A dotplot, matrix plot, or ranked marker table is acceptable.

children.name: Proportions table written
children.requirement: Write a cell-type proportion table stratified by timepoint.
children.grading_notes: Crew-level stratification is optional but welcome.

children.name: Annotation visualization written
children.requirement: Write at least one label-aware visualization such as a UMAP colored by final lineage.
children.grading_notes: The visualization may be raster or vector.

children.name: Differential expression job executes
children.requirement: Execute at least one longitudinal differential expression analysis without fatal error.
children.grading_notes: The contrast must be explicit in the output.

children.name: Ranked DEG tables written
children.requirement: Write ranked differential expression tables for the evaluated contrasts.
children.grading_notes: At least one machine-readable table is required.

children.name: Pathway analysis job executes
children.requirement: Execute pathway analysis on at least one ranked gene list without fatal error.
children.grading_notes: Open-source tools only.

children.name: Pathway summary written
children.requirement: Write a pathway summary artifact such as a table, plot, or markdown report.
children.grading_notes: The scorer should be able to inspect enrichment direction and significance.

children.name: DAR job executes
children.requirement: Execute differential accessibility analysis without fatal error.
children.grading_notes: Any valid peak-level or region-level implementation is acceptable.

children.name: Motif enrichment written
children.requirement: Write motif-enrichment results to disk.
children.grading_notes: The output should contain motif names and a ranking or significance measure.

children.name: Motif activity written
children.requirement: Write motif-activity or deviation outputs to disk.
children.grading_notes: A matrix, table, or summarized report is acceptable.

children.name: Gene activity written
children.requirement: Write gene-activity or promoter-accessibility outputs to disk.
children.grading_notes: The output must support comparison back to genes of interest.

children.name: Chromatin summary written
children.requirement: Write a concise summary artifact linking DARs, motifs, and RNA interpretation.
children.grading_notes: This can be a markdown note, JSON summary, or a combined table.

children.name: Retained nuclei count close to reference
children.requirement: The post-QC dataset retains a number of nuclei within 5% of the reference value of 151,411.
children.grading_notes: Strict leaf. Compare the retained-cell count to the hidden reference count.

children.name: All six timepoints present
children.requirement: The filtered dataset retains all six benchmark timepoints: L-92, L-44, L-3, R+1, R+45, and R+82.
children.grading_notes: Strict leaf. Exact timepoint coverage is required.

children.name: All four crew members present
children.requirement: The filtered dataset retains all four crew members after QC.
children.grading_notes: Strict leaf. A missing donor fails the leaf.

children.name: Multimodal retention rate close to reference
children.requirement: The proportion of filtered cells with both RNA and ATAC modalities available is within 5 percentage points of the hidden reference.
children.grading_notes: Compare against the reference filtered object, not the raw input.

children.name: Median RNA complexity close to reference
children.requirement: Median detected genes per retained cell are within 15% of the hidden reference median.
children.grading_notes: Use the filtered object and the benchmark's standardized metric definition.

children.name: Median mitochondrial fraction close to reference
children.requirement: Median mitochondrial fraction after filtering is within 2 absolute percentage points of the hidden reference.
children.grading_notes: This is intentionally strict because the paper uses an explicit mitochondrial cutoff.

children.name: Retention by timepoint close to reference
children.requirement: The retained-cell distribution across timepoints is within 10 percentage points of the hidden reference for every timepoint.
children.grading_notes: Compare normalized proportions, not raw counts alone.

children.name: Batch correction improves biological structure
children.requirement: The integrated representation improves biological grouping relative to the unintegrated baseline, with kBET rejection reduced by at least 0.10 while cluster-label ARI remains at or above 0.60.
children.grading_notes: Use the benchmark-standard unintegrated baseline and compare the integrated embedding on the same cells.

children.name: Nine major lineages recoverable
children.requirement: The final cluster structure supports recovery of the benchmark's nine immune lineages.
children.grading_notes: This leaf is evaluated after label normalization, not by raw cluster IDs.

children.name: Cluster label ARI above threshold
children.requirement: Ontology-normalized cluster labels achieve adjusted Rand index above 0.60 against hidden reference labels.
children.grading_notes: Strict numeric comparator leaf.

children.name: Cluster label NMI above threshold
children.requirement: Ontology-normalized cluster labels achieve normalized mutual information above 0.65 against hidden reference labels.
children.grading_notes: Strict numeric comparator leaf.

children.name: Embedding neighborhood overlap above threshold
children.requirement: The integrated embedding achieves k-nearest-neighbor overlap above 0.50 against the hidden reference graph.
children.grading_notes: Use the benchmark-specified k and neighbor-overlap implementation.

children.name: Batch-mixing metric within reference band
children.requirement: The integrated embedding reaches the hidden-reference iLISI band within 10% of the benchmark reference value.
children.grading_notes: Use iLISI as the default batch-mixing metric unless a task-specific override is documented.

children.name: Lineage marker separation recovered
children.requirement: Marker-based separation of the major T, NK, B, and monocyte compartments reaches one-vs-rest marker AUC of at least 0.80 against the hidden reference.
children.grading_notes: Use the benchmark marker panel and compute one-vs-rest AUCs on ontology-normalized compartments.

children.name: No dominant cluster collapse
children.requirement: No single cluster contains more than 35% of cells unless the hidden reference exceeds that proportion.
children.grading_notes: This guards against degenerate clusterings that still satisfy a few marker checks.

children.name: Ontology-normalized labels match reference vocabulary
children.requirement: Final labels normalize cleanly to the benchmark's eight Cell Ontology-backed lineage labels plus the explicit residual bucket `residual immune cells`.
children.grading_notes: Strict leaf. Unsupported free-text labels fail even if biologically plausible, but the residual bucket is permitted as a benchmark-specific non-ontology label.

children.name: Label overlap threshold met
children.requirement: After ontology normalization, the lineage label set achieves overlap@9 of 1.0 with the hidden reference vocabulary, including the explicit residual bucket.
children.grading_notes: Exact vocabulary recovery is required at the lineage level.

children.name: CD4 versus CD8 separation recovered
children.requirement: The submission separates CD4 and CD8 T-cell populations with F1 score above 0.75 against hidden reference labels.
children.grading_notes: Numeric-first comparator leaf.

children.name: CD14 versus CD16 monocyte separation recovered
children.requirement: The submission separates CD14 and CD16 monocyte populations with F1 score above 0.75 against hidden reference labels.
children.grading_notes: Numeric-first comparator leaf.

children.name: NK versus T-cell separation recovered
children.requirement: The submission separates NK cells from T cells with F1 score above 0.85 against hidden reference labels.
children.grading_notes: Numeric-first comparator leaf.

children.name: Cell-type proportion profile correlated with reference
children.requirement: The vector of lineage proportions across the nine immune lineages reaches Spearman rho above 0.85 versus the hidden reference.
children.grading_notes: Compare proportions after ontology normalization.

children.name: Major proportion stability pattern reproduced
children.requirement: The submission reproduces the reference pattern that major immune cell proportions remain broadly stable across timepoints, with maximum absolute proportion shift no greater than 0.10 for each non-residual lineage.
children.grading_notes: Compare timepoint-wise lineage proportions after ontology normalization.

children.name: Lineage marker overlap threshold met
children.requirement: Average overlap@20 between submission-derived lineage markers and hidden reference markers is at least 0.35 across the major benchmark lineages.
children.grading_notes: Use the same top-20 cutoff for every evaluated lineage.

children.name: FOXP3 increases in post-flight T cells
children.requirement: At least one benchmarked post-flight T-cell contrast shows FOXP3 with positive log2 fold change and adjusted p-value below 0.05.
children.grading_notes: Strict leaf anchored to a headline biological finding.

children.name: MHC class I suppression recovered
children.requirement: The submission recovers long-term post-flight suppression of MHC class I genes, with at least three of HLA-A, HLA-B, HLA-C, and HLA-E showing negative log2 fold change in the benchmark recovery contrast.
children.grading_notes: Use the benchmark recovery contrast defined in task metadata and require adjusted p-value below 0.05 for counted genes.

children.name: Spaceflight-signature gene overlap above threshold
children.requirement: The ranked spaceflight-signature gene list reaches rank-biased overlap of at least 0.60 against the hidden reference over the top 20 signed genes.
children.grading_notes: Use the benchmark primary post-flight contrast unless a task-specific override is documented.

children.name: Oxidative phosphorylation enrichment recovered
children.requirement: Oxidative phosphorylation is significantly enriched in the benchmark primary post-flight contrast, with normalized enrichment score greater than 0 and adjusted p-value below 0.05.
children.grading_notes: Use the pooled PBMC R+1 versus pooled pre-flight contrast unless a task-specific override is documented.

children.name: UV-response enrichment recovered
children.requirement: A UV-response pathway is significantly enriched in the expected contrast and direction.
children.grading_notes: Any accepted gene-set library is allowed if mapped to the benchmark pathway family.

children.name: Immune-program enrichment recovered
children.requirement: An immune-function pathway family highlighted by the paper is significantly enriched in the expected direction.
children.grading_notes: Ontology- or library-normalized pathway matching is allowed.

children.name: TCF21-related enrichment recovered
children.requirement: A TCF21-related gene program or target-set family is significantly enriched in the expected contrast and direction.
children.grading_notes: Pathway-family normalization is acceptable as long as it maps to the hidden reference family.

children.name: Pathway ranking overlap above threshold
children.requirement: The ranked pathway output for a benchmarked contrast reaches rank-biased overlap of at least 0.65 against the hidden reference over the top 15 pathway families.
children.grading_notes: Compare pathway-family-normalized outputs, not raw library-specific names alone.

children.name: Expected TF motifs recovered
children.requirement: The submission recovers at least four expected R+1-associated motifs from the benchmark motif set that includes SPIB, SPI1, CEBPD, SPIC, EHF, CEBPA, ELF3, IKZF1, EWSR1-FLI1, FOSL2, and KLF4.
children.grading_notes: Strict motif-membership leaf with family normalization allowed.

children.name: R+1 motif-activity peak reproduced
children.requirement: The submission reproduces the pattern that motif activity is strongest at R+1 and attenuates toward later recovery timepoints, with temporal profile Spearman rho of at least 0.80 against the hidden reference across the benchmark motif set.
children.grading_notes: Use the fixed benchmark motif panel and timepoint order.

children.name: DAR ranking overlap above threshold
children.requirement: The ranked DAR output for a benchmarked contrast reaches rank-biased overlap of at least 0.60 against the hidden reference over the top 50 regions or linked genes.
children.grading_notes: Use the benchmark primary post-flight contrast unless a task-specific override is documented.

children.name: RNA and ATAC sign concordance above threshold
children.requirement: The submission reaches sign concordance of at least 0.70 between RNA and ATAC-derived regulatory summaries across the benchmark key-gene panel.
children.grading_notes: Use the fixed benchmark panel and count a gene as concordant when RNA and ATAC effects share the same sign.

children.name: Regulatory signal consistency recovered
children.requirement: A benchmarked regulatory signal, such as FOXP3-associated accessibility or another hidden-reference immune-regulatory program, is recovered with the correct direction of change in at least 80% of the fixed benchmark panel.
children.grading_notes: Use the hidden reference panel selected by the benchmark author; the scorer should compare direction only, not raw magnitude.
